base.positive: Preserve the main subject(s) exactly as in the input image view. Do not change camera angle, orientation, or perspective; no rotation or guessing of unseen sides. Preserve exact proportions and silhouette; do not change shape or curvature. Preserve all parts and attachments: branches, handles, rings, jewelry, straps, lids, and any secondary elements; do not remove or thin them. If the object is held or supported (e.g. book in hand, item on plate), keep that support. If multiple objects are present, keep them all; do not remove secondary objects. Maintain their relative scale and placement. Only zoom in or out so that the main subject(s) are centered and occupy roughly three-quarters of the image height. Replace the background with a plain solid neutral color with strong contrast. Remove only background content (scene context) and any background text, logos, or watermarks. Preserve any text that is printed on or is part of the main subject(s). For window objects, keep the content visible through the window; do not replace it with solid background. Do not stylize; preserve geometry, materials, and original colors. Preserve original color saturation and hue; do not increase saturation or shift hue. Even, neutral lighting; crisp edges; high detail.
